step_by_step_thinking:
An asymptomatic tooth with deep caries on the occlusal surface and a radiolucent mass at the apex suggest chronic inflammation rather than acute infection. Therefore, the most probable cause would be chronic apical periodontitis.
\boxed{C} Based on the description of an asymptomatic tooth with deep caries and a radiopaque mass at the apex visible on X-ray, the scenario points towards ongoing inflammatory changes over time without signs of active acute infection.
